tasks.name: Get a 5kW residential quote with monthly payment under $120 and save it
tasks.description: Use the quote tool to configure a 5kW residential solar system for ZIP 94110, then select and save a quote where the estimated monthly payment is less than $120.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the main "Get a Quote" button in the hero section or header.
In the quote form, enter "94110" in the ZIP code field and select "Single-family home" as the property type.
Enter "1200" as the roof area in square feet and type "150" as the average monthly electric bill in dollars.
Open the "System size" dropdown and select the "5 kW" option.
Click the "Calculate quote" or "See estimate" button to view available plans.
On the results page, open the sort dropdown and choose "Monthly payment: Low to High".
From the sorted plans, click the first plan whose displayed estimated monthly payment is under "$120".
On the selected plan's detail page, click the "Save this quote" or "Add to quote list" button to store the quote.

tasks.name: Select a 6–8 kW residential package under $15,000 with at least 20-year warranty
tasks.description: Compare the first two residential packages in the 6–8 kW range and choose the one that offers a total price below $15,000 and a panel warranty of at least 20 years, then add it to the quote.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click the "Solar Packages" or "Systems & Pricing" link.
On the packages page, use the system type filter to select "Residential".
Adjust the system size filter so the minimum size is set to "6 kW" and the maximum size is set to "8 kW".
Open the sort dropdown and select "Total price: Low to High".
Ctrl-click (or use the equivalent action) on the first package in the list to open it in a new tab, then do the same for the second package in the list.
Switch to the first package tab, note the listed total price and panel warranty years, then switch to the second package tab and note the same details.

Between the two package tabs, identify the package that has a total price under "$15,000" and a panel warranty of at least "20 years" (if both qualify, choose the cheaper one), and on that package’s page click the "Add to quote" or "Request this package" button.

tasks.name: Book the earliest weekday 10:00 AM virtual consultation for ZIP 78704
tasks.description: Schedule a virtual solar consultation for ZIP 78704 at the earliest available weekday time slot at 10:00 AM and submit the booking form.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Schedule Consultation" or "Book an Appointment" button in the header or hero section.
On the consultation type step, select the "Virtual" or "Video call" option.
In the location section, enter "78704" in the ZIP code field and select "Home" or "Residential" as the property type.
Click the date picker to open the calendar and navigate to the earliest week that shows available appointments.
In the calendar, click the first available date that falls on a weekday (Monday through Friday).
In the time slot selector, choose the "10:00 AM" time slot for the selected date.
Fill in the contact form with a name (e.g., "Alex Rivera"), a phone number (e.g., "555-222-3344"), and an email address (e.g., "alex@example.com").
Click the "Confirm appointment" or "Book consultation" button to submit the appointment request.

tasks.name: Save the most recent federal solar tax credit article from the last 12 months
tasks.description: Search the blog for federal solar tax credit information, filter to articles from the last 12 months, then save the most recent relevant article to a reading list with a specific tag.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
From the main navigation, hover over or click "Resources" and then select the "Blog" or "Articles" section.
On the blog page, click the search field and type "federal solar tax credit", then submit the search.
Use the date filter controls to set the date range to show only posts from the "Last 12 months".
Open the sort dropdown and select "Most recent" or "Newest first".
From the filtered and sorted list, click the first article whose title includes the phrase "Federal Solar Tax Credit" or a similar reference to the tax credit.
On the article page, click the "Save", "Bookmark", or "Add to reading list" icon or button.
In the tagging or label field that appears, type "Incentives 2024" as the tag or label and submit it (e.g., by pressing Enter or clicking an "Add tag" button).

tasks.name: Create a savings scenario with at least $1,500 estimated annual savings and save it
tasks.description: Use the savings calculator for ZIP 85001 with a $200 monthly bill and adjust the system size until the estimated annual savings reach at least $1,500, then save the configuration with a specific name.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Savings Calculator" or "See Your Savings" link in the main navigation or hero section.
In the calculator form, enter "85001" in the ZIP code field and type "200" in the monthly electric bill field in dollars.
Set the roof orientation dropdown to "South-facing" and adjust the shading control to "Low (0–25%)" if available.
Set the system size input or slider to "6 kW".
Click the "Calculate" or "Estimate savings" button to see the savings estimate.

If the displayed estimated annual savings is less than "$1,500", drag the system size slider upward in 1 kW increments (e.g., 7 kW, then 8 kW, etc.) and click the "Calculate" or "Update" button after each change until the estimated annual savings reaches at least "$1,500".
Once the estimated annual savings is at least "$1,500", click the "Save this configuration", "Save scenario", or similar button.
In the scenario name field, type "Target $1500+ savings" and click the "Save" or "Done" button to store the scenario.

tasks.name: Contact a top-rated installer within 25 miles of ZIP 30301 with 4.5+ stars and 50+ reviews
tasks.description: Find a solar installer near ZIP 30301 who is within 25 miles, has at least a 4.5-star rating and 50+ reviews, then open their profile and send a contact message.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Find Installers", "Installers Near You", or similar link in the main navigation.
In the installer search field, type "30301" as the ZIP code and submit the search.
Use the distance filter to set the search radius to "Within 25 miles".
Set the rating filter to show installers with "4.5 stars & up".
Set the reviews filter or input to show only installers with "50+" reviews.
Open the sort dropdown and select "Rating: High to Low".
From the filtered and sorted list, click on the first installer card to open the installer’s profile page.
On the installer profile page, click the "Contact this installer" or "Request a quote" button.

In the contact form, enter the name "Jordan Lee", the phone number "555-123-7890", select "Phone" as the preferred contact method, type the message "Interested in a 7 kW residential solar installation in the next 3 months", and click the "Send message" or "Submit" button.

tasks.name: Design a residential system with 16×400W panels and at least 10 kWh battery storage
tasks.description: Use the system builder to create a residential design with asphalt shingle roof, 16 panels of 400W each, and a battery storage capacity of at least 10 kWh, then proceed to the summary page.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
In the main navigation, click the "Design Your System", "System Builder", or similar tool link.
On the first step of the builder, select "Residential" as the system type and click the "Continue" or "Next" button.
On the roof details step, choose "Asphalt shingle" as the roof type and select "Medium (25–35°)" as the roof pitch if that option is available.
On the panel selection step, choose the panel model that is labeled "400W" (e.g., "400W premium") and set the quantity field to "16" panels.
Scroll to the storage or battery section and toggle the "Include battery storage" or similar option to the ON position.
From the battery options, select a battery capacity option that is at least "10 kWh" (for example, choose "10 kWh" if available; if not, select the next higher capacity).
Navigate to the inverter step and select "String inverter" from the inverter type options.
Click the "View system summary", "Review design", or similar button to open the system summary page with the configured design.

tasks.name: Choose a financing option with under $3,000 upfront and under $150/month and save a pre-application
tasks.description: Compare financing options for an $18,000 project, filter to options with an upfront payment below $3,000 and monthly payments below $150, select the lowest-APR option from the filtered list, and save a pre-application.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Financing" or "Payment Options" link in the main navigation.
On the financing page, click the "Compare options" or "Start comparison" button.
In the project information form, enter "18000" as the project cost in dollars, type "60601" as the ZIP code, select the credit score range "680–719", and continue to the results.
On the financing results page, use the filters to set the maximum upfront payment to "$3,000" and the maximum monthly payment to "$150".
Open the sort dropdown and choose "APR: Low to High".
From the filtered and sorted options, click the first financing option card to open its details.
On the financing option detail page, click the "Apply", "Start application", or similar button.
In the application form, enter "18000" as the loan amount and select "15 years" from the term length dropdown.
Click the "Save pre-application", "Save and finish later", or equivalent button to store the financing application draft.
